items.title: Three new bulk-billing GP clinics opening in Canberra

children.content: Three new bulk-billing GP clinics are opening in Canberra, but questions remain about how they will be funded and staffed long-term.

avatar.alt: Niki Burnside is a journalist and digital producer based in Canberra.

items.title: Man who stabbed wife in front of daughter found guilty of attempted murder

children.content: An ACT Supreme Court jury has found a Canberra man guilty of attempted murder after he stabbed his wife during a violent attack in the family's Gungahlin home.

image.altText: The ACT Supreme Court towards dusk.

items.title: Museum's macabre merchandise, outback evidence from the Chamberlain trial

children.content: Forty years ago, the chance discovery of a bloodied matinee jacket unravelled a murder conviction. Now, that evidence lives inside a national museum's collection that's too sensitive for public display.

image.altText: A black and white photo of a couple outside court, they are holding up a large photo of Lindy Chamberlain holding baby Azaria.

items.title: Does Pauline Hanson's claim of growing support in the ACT pass the pub test?

children.content: The conventional wisdom is that Canberra is a largely left-leaning city, but the national capital is now among places in One Nation's sights as support for the party surges across the country.

items.title: Hikers lost near Kosciuszko found by artificial intelligence drone

children.content: Rescuers were able to locate the hikers less than five hours after they were reported missing.

image.altText: A grey image with a red dot.

items.title: 'Insurance policy' hoping to protect quolls if bird flu arrives in ACT

children.content: A population of endangered eastern quolls is being protected by a sanctuary in Canberra, but the threat of bird flu could mean that protection does not go far enough.

image.altText: Eastern quoll at Mulligans Flat Woodland Sanctuary

items.title: 'Systemic issue': Abuse sees Canberra referees quitting after a year

children.content: Canberra soccer match officials say referee abuse is a "systemic issue" across all sporting codes, with increasingly hostile behaviour from spectators, players and coaches causing 44 per cent of Capital Football's referees to leave their roles after a year.

items.title: 'I've got all the symptoms': Matty Robbins was a hero of the AFL. Now he's suffering

children.content: More than 30 professional and amateur Aussie Rules players have been diagnosed with CTE, revealing that the brain trauma crisis is striking all levels of the game.

articles.title: Chelsea Randall retires from AFLW for 'issues related to concussion'

children.content: Adelaide Crows star Chelsea Randall has announced her retirement from the AFLW, with the club citing concussion as a major factor in her decision.

items.title: Allan government under pressure amid latest Big Build corruption allegations

items.title: Health minister stops short of requesting coronial inquest into baby's death

children.content: WA's health minister says she would support a coronial inquest into the death of an eight-month-old girl at Kalgoorlie Health Campus, but says it is a decision for the state coroner.

image.altText: A baby sitting in hospital bed looking at camera

items.title: France records 1,000 excess daily deaths as record heatwave broken by severe storms

children.content: More than 150 million Europeans swelter in "extreme heat", which scientists say has been the worst the continent has ever recorded.

items.title: Family who spent $250,000 on IVF welcomes new rebate scheme in SA

children.content: A South Australian couple who spent $250,000 on fertility treatments say the government's IVF rebate will make a "huge difference" for people trying to conceive.

items.title: Expanded World Cup called 'vulgar and ordinary' by Ghana coach. Is that fair?

children.content: Ghana's coach Carlos Queiroz has lashed out at FIFA's expanded 48-team World Cup, despite his team progressing. Is his criticism justified as the tournament moves into the knockout stage?

articles.title: Quick hits: Canada's emotional match-winner and scenes in rural Kansas

children.content: Canada's heroes tread new ground, South Korea's extreme reaction to going out and scenes in Algeria's home away from home. Here's the five quick hits from the World Cup.

articles.title: Canada 'heroes' score stunning injury time winner to down South Africa

items.title: Ben Stokes's retirement a perfect example of his main character energy

children.content: Ben Stokes has been the centre of England's cricketing universe for 15 years. In announcing his retirement mid-match, he highlighted how damaging that main character energy can be.

items.title: Les Mills, NZ Olympian and founder of global fitness brand, dies aged 91

children.content: The New Zealand Olympian who opened an Auckland gym in 1968 that grew into an international group fitness brand has died aged 91.

items.title: NASA to bump telescope back into space to avoid it falling to Earth

items.title: US and Iran to halt strikes as talks set to continue

children.content: The US and Iran will stand down on strikes and vessels will be allowed to move freely, as technical talks are slated to continue on all areas of the memorandum of understanding.

items.title: Renewed Gulf hostilities put markets on edge, ASX inches up

items.title: Last week was a turning point inside the Liberal party

children.content: While Angus Taylor grapples with the Coalition's dilemma of how to keep the centre while diverting voters away from Pauline Hanson's party, Andrew Hastie has been talking tough on One Nation.

items.title: Baby, young boys pulled alive from Venezuela earthquake rubble

children.content: The three children are among at least 33 people rescued during a weekend multinational operation that continued searching for survivors, while thousands remained unaccounted-for.

items.title: Crisafulli confirms children in state care have slept in government offices

image.altText: david crisafulli in a suit

items.title: Williams takes aim at anti-doping measures ahead of Wimbledon return

children.content: As she prepares for her All England Club comeback, Serena Williams claims the tennis anti-doping protocol is "unprofessional" and "unreasonable".

image.altText: Serena Williams walks to the training grounds at the All England Lawn Tennis and Croquet Club in London.

items.title: Power tools used in 'stupid' and potentially lethal acts of Stobie pole vandalism

children.content: Members of the public were put at "severe risk" of electrocution after four Stobie poles were vandalised in Adelaide's north in the past month, causing some to fall and expose high voltage electrical lines.

items.title: Maroons recall veteran for Origin decider after Blues' bombshell

image.altText: Reece Walsh hugs Josh Papali'i as Pat Carrigan looks on on the sidelines of State of Origin.

items.title: 'Unlucky being born as Iranian': Diaspora's social fabric under strain

children.content: Iranian Australians are grappling with mistrust, harassment and isolation within a community deeply traumatised by an oppressive regime.
